layman_headline: Extreme fear grips the crypto market — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is in deep fear territory, with Bitcoin struggling around $60,000 and traders loading up on downside bets. Most altcoins have been beaten down hard over the past week, some losing 30-80% of their value. While Solana stands out as showing relative strength with a 7% daily bounce, the overall chart structure and market mood suggest it's too early to commit capital. We're watching for a clearer bottom signal before deploying.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is still falling and fear is intensifying. A better entry point for quality coins like Solana should emerge if Bitcoin stabilizes or the put-call ratio normalizes. Don't try to catch a falling knife.
expert_reasoning: None of the swing candidates present a compelling risk-adjusted entry right now. TAC and VELVET are massively extended (RSI 68-70, +197% and +236% weekly) with no pullback entry. GWEI has the best chart bias (+1.40) with a Morning Star pattern, but the R:R is only 1.01x and RSI at 62 after a 21% weekly run leaves little upside margin. KITE has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows — a falling knife. WLD dropped 31% but shows bearish marubozu candles confirming selling pressure. SOL is the most liquid, fundamentally sound candidate with approaching-oversold RSI (41.75), but its chart bias is still -0.70 with price below all MAs. The macro backdrop — Extreme Fear at 12, worsening trend, BTC put-call at 1-year highs, and high BTC dominance — strongly argues for patience. We need either a capitulation flush or a confirmed reversal pattern before entering.
risk_factors: Bitcoin put-call ratio at 1-year high suggests traders expect a drop to $55K, which would drag all alts significantly lower
Fear & Greed at 12 and worsening — could fall further before capitulation bottom forms
BTC dominance at 55.6% means capital is flowing out of alts into Bitcoin; alt recovery may lag significantly
watch_list: Bitcoin price action around $58K-$60K — a breakdown below $58K would signal another leg down and invalidate all alt setups
Fear & Greed index stabilization or uptick — any reading above 20 with an improving trend would signal potential bottom
SOL's ability to hold $66 support on any retest — if it holds, a confirmed double bottom would be a strong entry signal
market_context: The crypto market is in Extreme Fear (12/100, worsening) with Bitcoin balancing on the $60K level while derivatives markets show the heaviest bearish positioning in a year. High BTC dominance at 55.6% confirms capital is fleeing altcoins, making this a dangerous environment for new alt positions.
context_banner: Extreme Fear at 12 with worsening sentiment and record bearish derivatives positioning demands patience — the best swing trade right now is no trade at all.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 12/100 with a worsening weekly trend (avg 15.7) indicates the market has not yet found a bottom — historically, buying works best when fear stabilizes or begins reversing.
Bitcoin put-call ratio hitting a 1-year high shows institutional traders are aggressively hedging downside, suggesting smart money expects further weakness before any recovery.
Bitcoin's $60K breakdown setting up a 'volatility shock' warns that the current support level is fragile and a decisive break lower could trigger rapid selling.
SOL RSI at 41.75 is approaching oversold but not there yet — ideal swing entry would be on RSI touching 30-35 or a confirmed reversal candle pattern at support.

SOL's 7.25% daily bounce on strong volume ($4.25B) shows buying interest, but price remains below all major moving averages with a bearish chart bias of -0.70, suggesting this is a relief rally rather than a trend reversal.
